title: pkg/systemd: don't require LISTEN_FDNAMES for socket activation
body:
LISTEN_FDNAMES is optional, the docs for sd_listen_fds() says:

    This information is read from the $LISTEN_FDNAMES variable, which
    **may** contain a colon-separated list of names.

emphasis mine (indeed, the cited coreos code also suggests it is optional).

This actually results in bug, since the default
/contrib/systemd/system/podman.socket file doesn't set a
FileDescriptorName=. podman when run with this systemd configuration
*always* starts in unix socket mode since SocketActivated() will return
false because the name is missing.

The bug is a race with a very small window: between when podman does the
unlink() and when it re-binds the socket later in the code, requests made
during this time will fail since nothing is listening. There's another
small race when the service stops and systemd realizes it and starts
listening again.

However, small this window we managed to hit it :).

Let's fix this by ignoring LISTEN_FDNAMES. Since the code in
cmd/podman/system/service_abi.go:restService() ignores this value anyway
when setting up the socket activated stuff, there's no real loss here.

Signed-off-by: Tycho Andersen <tycho@tycho.pizza>

comments.body: PR is complaining about a lack of tests. I don't believe we have existing tests for socket activation (though @edsantiago may correct me there), so you will probably have to add `[NO TESTS NEEDED]` to the PR title and re-push.
Thanks, I think it shouldn't be too hard to write some tests for this, so I'll go ahead and do that later today. Have to run for now though.

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe,mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Quote from `man systemd.socket` (regarding `FileDescriptorName=` in Systemd version 248) :
_If this setting is not used, the file descriptor name defaults to the name of the socket unit, including its .socket suffix_

One way to interpret that sentence is that LISTEN_FDNAMES would always be set.
(I'm a bit confused about whether LISTEN_FDNAMES is optional or not)

Anyway if we assume that LISTEN_FDNAMES is optional maybe this code needs to be adjusted too?
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/4b3fc0c67a1eb4970b55828e1e1ac1c9679ef656/pkg/rootless/rootless_linux.c#L314
The if-statement in the following lines does not treat the environment variable LISTEN_FDNAMES as optional.
Another thing that I noticed is that `strdup(listen_fdnames)` may be given a NULL pointer. I've not verified it but it seems to be that way just by reading the code.

> One way to interpret that sentence is that LISTEN_FDNAMES would always be set. (I'm a bit confused about whether LISTEN_FDNAMES is optional or not)

All I can say is that on systemd 219 (centos 7) it is not set, and that some man pages seem to think it is optional while others do not :)

I pushed a basic smoke test for the patch as-is, though.
I'm not sure that the code that I mentioned (in _rootless_linux.c_) is directly related to this PR. I'll need to investigate it a bit more. I could open up a new issue or PR if needed.
Sorry,l just realized you had asked for a rebase :). Done.
